insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The GraphQL Admin API can be used to fetch the channelId for a specific sales channel.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will have more accurate and sales channel-specific data available to them through apps using the updated Storefront API.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying sales channel-specific data, which is essential for merchants with multiple sales channels.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for apps storing cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId argument for applying sales channel context to queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: ExchangeLineItem fields now include productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components in cart line item data.
CLI 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 improves command metadata and build error handling.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects.

updates.title: skeleton@2026.4.5
updates.summary: skeleton@2026.4.5 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects.

updates.title: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target
updates.summary: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target for observing POS events and running background logic.

recap.headline: Shopify updates require mTLS certificates, Hydrogen patches, and App Store requirement changes

themes.title: Security Updates
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps storing cardholder data must comply. Additionally, Hydrogen patches include improvements to error handling and command metadata.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI Updates
themes.detail: Hydrogen patches include non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and the ability to bypass confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade. The CLI has been updated to version 4.5.1, with new store commands and improvements to hydrogen and create-hydrogen.

themes.title: API and App Store Changes
themes.detail: The Storefront API now supports the channelId argument in the @inContext directive, and new fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type. The App Store has updated requirements, including the use of unique app names, and POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components.

actions.text: Update apps to use mTLS certificates for card deposit endpoint
Review and comply with updated App Store requirements
Test and deploy Hydrogen patches and CLI updates
